description:
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Component Expansion

Build hx-accordion: collapsible content sections for FAQ, expandable content. Progressive enhancement with <details>/<summary> fallback. Single and multi-expand modes.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Single and multi-expand modes
- [ ] Animated open/close transitions
- [ ] Progressive enhancement fallback
- [ ] Keyboard: Enter/Space to toggle
- [ ] CSS parts: item, trigger, content, icon
- [ ] Events: hx-expand, hx-collapse
- [ ] ARIA: expanded/hidden states
- [ ] <5KB gzipped

**Complexity:** medium
title: Implement hx-accordion component

descriptionHistory.description:
**Milestone:** Phase 2: Component Expansion

Build hx-accordion: collapsible content sections for FAQ, expandable content. Progressive enhancement with <details>/<summary> fallback. Single and multi-expand modes.

**Files to Modify:**
- packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Single and multi-expand modes
- [ ] Animated open/close transitions
- [ ] Progressive enhancement fallback
- [ ] Keyboard: Enter/Space to toggle

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 8**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/hx-accordion-item.styles.ts`:
- Around line 9-11: The stylesheet is using literal fallback values instead of
token-only cascades; update the hx-accordion-item styles to remove hardcoded
fallbacks (e.g., replace occurrences like var(--hx-accordion-border-color,
`#dee2e6`) and var(--hx-font-family-sans, sans-serif) with token-only var(...)
usages) and remove numeric literals in properties (border-width, spacing,
durations, font-size, line-height) so every value solely consumes a design token
(e.g., use --hx-border-width-thin, --hx-accordion-border-color, --hx-font-size,
--hx-spacing-*, --hx-transition-duration-* tokens directly). Locate and fix all
instances in this module where literals appear (including the
border-bottom/font-family line and the other ranges called out) so no hardcoded
colors, spacing, typography, or timing remain and each var(...) has no literal
fallback.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/hx-accordion-item.ts`:
- Around line 135-153: The region element with role="region" is unlabeled; give
it an accessible name by adding an id on the summary trigger and referencing
that id from the region via aria-labelledby. Update the template where <summary
part="trigger" ...> is rendered (the trigger/summary element used with
_handleSummaryClick/_handleKeyDown) to include a stable id (e.g., compute or use
a component-scoped id like this._summaryId or this.id + '-summary'), and set
aria-labelledby="${thatId}" on the inner div with role="region" (the element
with part="content" / class="content") so screen readers announce the summary as
the region label.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-accordion/hx-accordion.stories.ts`:
- Line 144: Replace each fixed "await ne

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: `aria-hidden="false"` is explicitly discouraged in WCAG guidance — some assistive technologies ignore the `false` value inconsistently. The correct pattern is to omit the attribute entirely when content is visible (not hidden). Lit's `ifDefined` + `nothing` can conditionally apply `aria-hidden` only when `true`.

The non-null assertion `secondTrigger!` on a `shadowRoot?.querySelector` result bypasses null safety. If the shadow root hasn't rendered yet, this throws at runtime. The guard `await expect(secondTrigger).toBeTruthy()` on the previous line helps but doesn't satisfy TypeScript null safety — the click still uses the unchecked `!`. Should add an explicit null check before calling `userEvent.click`.

This is a trivial nitpick about using `setTimeout` in story play functions. The `new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 50))` pattern is a standard, widely-used way to flush async Lit updates in tests/stories. Adding `waitFor` from `@storybook/test` would add complexity and a new dependency for no meaningful benefit. The existing hx-button and hx-card stories use the same timing pattern. Not worth the change.

`font-family` set on `.accordion` (a shadow DOM element) does not inherit into slotted `hx-accordion-item` elements because slotted content lives in the light DOM and inherits from its own parent chain, not from shadow host internals. This declaration has no effect on accordion items. Should be on `:host` so it properly applies to the custom element itself.

`@property({ type: String, reflect: true })` with a union type `'single' | 'multi'` is a standard, correct Lit pattern. Lit uses `type: String` for attribute serialization — the TypeScript union narrows the type at compile time, and the runtime behavior for invalid values gracefully defaults (the single-expand handler's `if (this.mode !== 'single') return` check handles any invalid value). No change needed.
